title: Packages, Repositories, Dependencies, and Software Provenance · Incident Repair Bay
summary: Install or update a required tool while preserving repository trust, version evidence, rollback options, and the boundary between system and project dependencies. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a distribution-aware package change record and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case p…
objectives: Distinguish repositories, metadata, packages, dependencies, installed files, scripts, and transaction history.
Query package origin, version, architecture, files, dependencies, reverse dependencies, and candidate changes before mutation.
Evaluate repository ownership, signing keys, transport, scope, pinning, and update policy before adding a source.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Order the regression proof
steps.instruction: Order the Package Rollback evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Package Rollback assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Host proposes lab-tool 4.4.1 from a signed distribution repository and 4.5.0 from an unverified script. Inspect origin, dependencies, installed files, service impact, and rollback before choosing. Competency probe: Plan configuration preservation, cache use, downgrade constraints, service impact, and verification for package rollback.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Package Rollback has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the Package Rollback precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Host proposes lab-tool 4.4.1 from a signed distribution repository and 4.5.0 from an unverified script. Inspect origin, dependencies, installed files, service impact, and rollback before choosing. Competency probe: Plan configuration preservation, cache use, downgrade constraints, service impact, and verification for package rollback.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Uninstalling a package always restores the machine to its previous state.

options.text: Capture user, directory, environment, target state, and authoritative command behavior for the debug Package Rollback case
Predict shell parsing, expansion, permissions, side effects, exit status, and safety boundary for the debug Package Rollback case
Run the smallest simulated observation or reversible change and retain exact output for the debug Package Rollback case
Verify changed state, failure path, rollback, and an accessible operations handoff for the debug Package Rollback case

options.text: The Package Manager Model record says a distribution-aware package change record looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. This does not resolve inspect the failure record.

For inspect the failure record, apply Package Manager Model without its stated preconditions, omit the a distribution-aware package change record trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Package Manager Model for a distribution-aware package change record.
The record names the assumption, applies Package Manager Model, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a safe Linux operations investigation headed "# Evidence: linux-package-rollback-4fvz29" with a concrete hypothesis, one bounded simulator command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Package Rollback into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: linux-package-rollback-4fvz29
# hypothesis: [replace with one falsifiable cause]
# command: choose one bounded simulator observation
# observation: [replace with the result that changes the next decision]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Package Rollback structural check is only one gate. a distribution-aware package change record must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

content.title: Evidence defense

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
